summary: Israel’s military reported killing a senior Hezbollah engineering commander and destroying a rocket launcher targeting its forces in southern Lebanon. Clashes erupted between Amal movement supporters and Hezbollah in Saida, Lebanon, while a Zionist‑made Haraz‑450 UAV was shot down over western Bekaa. The Iranian Revolutionary Guard claimed to have struck two U.S. warships in the Gulf of Oman.
key_points: Israel’s air force eliminated Hezbollah’s engineering unit commander, "Abd Harb," and destroyed a rocket launcher aimed at IDF troops in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200992).
Hezbollah and Amal movement fighters engaged in street fighting in Saida, southern Lebanon, over the placement of Hezbollah rocket launchers among residential houses (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72602).
A Zionist‑manufactured Haraz‑450 drone was downed over western Bekaa, Lebanon, by a ground‑to‑air missile (Source: @naya_foriraq/77108).
Israeli reports cite 650 strikes in southern Lebanon during the past week and the killing of five militants in Gaza (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
The Iranian Revolutionary Guard announced missile attacks on two U.S. warships in the Gulf of Oman (Source: @pkpoi/142437).
Lebanese President Michel Aoun publicly urged Iran to cease interference in Lebanon and called for diplomatic resolution with Israel (Source: @VahidOnline/75948).
U.S. officials are preparing a draft IAEA resolution to condemn Iran ahead of the next IAEA Board meeting (Source: @SharghDaily/164044).

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s air force eliminated Hezbollah’s senior engineering commander and a rocket launcher targeting its troops in southern Lebanon. Clashes erupted in Saida over Hezbollah rocket placements, and a Haraz‑450 UAV was shot down over western Bekaa. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard claimed missile strikes on two U.S. warships in the Gulf of Oman.

points: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard announced it launched missiles that struck two American warships in the Gulf of Oman, indicating a direct escalation against U.S. naval assets (Source: @pkpoi/142437).
U.S. diplomats are drafting an IAEA resolution to condemn Iran, reflecting heightened diplomatic pressure ahead of the agency’s Board meeting (Source: @SharghDaily/164044).
Lebanese President Michel Aoun publicly requested Iran to stop intervening in Lebanon’s affairs, emphasizing diplomatic over military solutions (Source: @VahidOnline/75948).
Iranian media reports claim the Revolutionary Guard’s missile attacks, but no independent verification is provided in the source messages.
The IDF reported 650 airstrikes in southern Lebanon over the last week, targeting Hezbollah positions and infrastructure (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
A senior Hezbollah engineering commander, identified as Abd Harb, was killed and a rocket launcher aimed at IDF forces was destroyed by the Israeli Air Force (Source: @New_security8200/200992).
Five militants were reported killed in Gaza during the same period, indicating ongoing operations on multiple fronts (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
Israeli forces suffered injuries from a suspected aerial target in southern Lebanon, with at least one combat officer wounded severely (Source: @pkpoi/142438).
The IDF weekly summary noted the elimination of five significant militants in Gaza (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
More than 60 arrests were reported in the West Bank, reflecting intensified security operations (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
Hezbollah and Amal movement fighters clashed in Saida, southern Lebanon, over the deployment of Hezbollah rocket launchers within residential neighborhoods (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72602).
Lebanese army units moved into Saida to calm the situation following the street fighting (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72602).
A Zionist‑made Haraz‑450 UAV was shot down over the western Bekaa region by a ground‑to‑air missile (Source: @naya_foriraq/77108).
Israeli aircraft were observed conducting high‑alert flights over multiple villages in southern Lebanon (Source: @redlinkleb/21949).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard claimed missile attacks on two U.S. warships in the Gulf of Oman, suggesting a possible widening of hostilities in the region (Source: @pkpoi/142437).

key_developments: Israel’s air force eliminated Hezbollah engineering commander Abd Harb and destroyed a rocket launcher targeting IDF troops in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200992).
Hezbollah and Amal supporters engaged in armed clashes in Saida, Lebanon, over the placement of rocket launchers among civilian houses (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72602).
A Haraz‑450 UAV, described as Zionist‑made, was downed by a surface‑to‑air missile over western Bekaa, Lebanon (Source: @naya_foriraq/77108).
The IDF reported conducting 650 strikes in southern Lebanon during the past week (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
Five militants were killed in Gaza according to Israeli weekly operational summary (Source: @idf_telegram/24157).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard announced missile strikes on two U.S. warships in the Gulf of Oman (Source: @pkpoi/142437).
U.S. officials are preparing a draft IAEA resolution to condemn Iran ahead of the agency’s upcoming Board meeting (Source: @SharghDaily/164044).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to field engineered explosive devices and rocket launchers, prompting targeted Israeli strikes that have eliminated senior personnel. Clashes between Hezbollah and Amal in Saida reveal intra‑Lebanese factional volatility that could destabilize southern Lebanon. Iran’s claimed missile attacks on U.S. naval assets raise the risk of broader regional escalation, especially as diplomatic pressure mounts through a prospective IAEA resolution.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese President Michel Aoun’s appeal for Iranian non‑interference underscores internal pressure to limit external meddling. Israeli high‑alert air operations over southern Lebanon indicate a sustained campaign to degrade Hezbollah capabilities. International actors, notably the United States, are leveraging diplomatic channels at the IAEA to isolate Iran, while maintaining a watchful stance on potential maritime confrontations in the Gulf of Oman.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah’s engineering units should focus on supply chains for rocket launchers and improvised explosives. The Saida confrontation suggests possible future flashpoints where militia rivalries intersect with civilian areas. Iranian Revolutionary Guard missile capabilities warrant close surveillance of Gulf shipping lanes, and any escalation could trigger U.S. naval responses. Continued Israeli strike activity in southern Lebanon may lead to higher civilian displacement and cross‑border retaliation.
